title: sm-max-tokens-truncation-repeat
description:
This is a continuation of sm-max-tokens-truncation-repeat. In the current window, the SM persona again produced two consecutive failed runs with explicit truncation signatures at 2026-05-29T13:00:34.047512+00:00 and 2026-05-29T13:00:34.064659+00:00. Both errors state that JSON parsing failed because output hit a max_tokens limit with finish_reason=length, which is a clear model-output truncation pattern rather than an ambiguous story-domain failure. The failures were bracketed by successful SM runs immediately before and after, indicating an intermittent but recurring factory-side output-formatting issue rather than a total outage.

Observed impact in this specific window is low: the runs are null-story SM activity, duration is only milliseconds, and recorded cost for these attempts is 0.0. However, the diagnostic clarity is high because the error text names the failure mode directly, and this pattern has already appeared in prior concerns. That makes the current note evidence of persistence rather than an isolated blip.

Other signals in the window do not materially change the assessment. The only completed tick was normal at 2.454s with no errors or still-running work, spend was flat, and the queue skew toward blocked_tests_need_clarification remained unchanged rather than worsening. Several additional failures appeared in sandbox-style null-story test contexts with "tests not green after run", but those are separate and ambiguous relative to live factory operation.

Plausible explanations supported by the evidence are limited to SM response generation or its output contract at current settings: the model is sometimes emitting incomplete JSON when stopped at token limits, and downstream parsing then fails. Because the pattern is clearly named in the run errors and has now recurred across summarizer windows, L3 judgment would add value in assessing whether this is a persistent persona/model-settings problem.

evidence.summary_excerpt: Main new signal was a pair of SM run failures where the error text explicitly says JSON parsing failed because output hit max_tokens with finish_reason=length; surrounding SM attempts before and after succeeded.

evidence.excerpt: SM success immediately before failures: success=true attempt_n=815 model=deepseek/deepseek-chat tokens_in=20 tokens_out=2068.
success=false persona=sm attempt_n=816 error="json parse failed at max_tokens=65536 finish_reason=length: Unterminated string starting at: line 1 column 9 (char 8)" tokens_out=122880
success=false persona=sm attempt_n=817 error="json parse failed at max_tokens=8192 finish_reason=length: Unterminated string starting at: line 1 column 7 (char 6)"
SM success immediately after failures: success=true attempt_n=818 model=deepseek/deepseek-chat.
{"noteworthy":"Two SM failures are clearly diagnosable infrastructure/model-output truncation errors (json parse failed at max_tokens with finish_reason=length)."}
{"noteworthy":"SM also had 2 closely spaced truncation failures, reinforcing the max_tokens issue."}

escalation_reason: Recurring SM max-tokens JSON truncation pattern has persisted across multiple summarizer windows, including two consecutive failed SM runs at 2026-05-29T13:00:34Z in this window. The error text explicitly names the cause (finish_reason=length at max_tokens), so L3 judgment is needed to assess persistent SM persona/model-setting behavior before the same failure mode burns more runs.
